title: chenopodium iranicum
intro: Chenopodium iranicum, also known as Iranian lamb's quarters, is a plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family and is native to Iran. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving liver function. Chenopodium iranicum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including colds, flu, and digestive problems.
